name: Ubiquiti UniFi Switch Mission Critical
title: Ubiquiti UniFi Switch Mission Critical
title_extended: Ubiquiti UniFi Switch Mission Critical – 8x PoE ports, 368Wh Li-Ion battery, Layer 2 switching
short_description: Mission Critical Switch, 1x GbE RJ45, 4xGbE PoE+, 4x GbE PoE++, 1.3" touchscreen, 368Wh Li-Ion battery
description: The Ubiquiti UniFi Switch Mission Critical (USW-Mission-Critical) combines advanced networking with integrated power backup to ensure your network stays online during power interruptions. Equipped with a built-in 368Wh lithium-ion battery, this switch delivers uninterrupted Power over Ethernet (PoE) to up to eight devices, including high-powered access points and surveillance cameras. Featuring 4 GbE PoE+ ports and 4 GbE PoE++ ports with a total PoE budget of 120W, it supports devices requiring up to 64W per port. Two 230V AC outputs provide additional power options for connected equipment. This switch includes a dedicated GbE uplink for seamless network integration. Designed for critical applications such as enterprise environments, emergency response, and service providers, it minimizes downtime and guarantees continuous connectivity even during outages, securing your essential network infrastructure.

detailed_description.heading: Uninterrupted Power
detailed_description.text: The Ubiquiti UniFi Switch Mission Critical (USW-Mission-Critical) combines advanced networking with integrated power backup to ensure your network stays online during power interruptions. Equipped with a built-in 368Wh lithium-ion battery, this switch delivers uninterrupted Power over Ethernet (PoE) to up to eight devices, including high-powered access points and surveillance cameras. Featuring 4 GbE PoE+ ports and 4 GbE PoE++ ports with a total PoE budget of 120W, it supports devices requiring up to 64W per port. Two 230V AC outputs provide additional power options for connected equipment. This switch includes a dedicated GbE uplink for seamless network integration. Designed for critical applications such as enterprise environments, emergency response, and service providers, it minimizes downtime and guarantees continuous connectivity even during outages, securing your essential network infrastructure.

detailed_description.heading: Powerful Layer 2 Features
detailed_description.text: With a high switching capacity of 18 Gbps and non-blocking throughput of 9 Gbps, the USW-Mission-Critical delivers exceptional Layer 2 switching performance ideal for demanding network environments. It supports key features including VLAN tagging, port isolation, Storm Control to mitigate broadcast storms, IGMP Snooping for efficient multicast handling, and link aggregation to increase bandwidth and redundancy. The switch includes a 1.3" touchscreen LCM display for easy monitoring and configuration on site. Full compatibility with UniFi Network software (version 6.2.26 and later) enables centralized management and detailed network visibility through a user-friendly dashboard. This powerful combination of hardware and software gives IT administrators full control over network traffic while ensuring reliability and scalability for mission-critical deployments.

detailed_description.heading: Rugged and Reliable Design
detailed_description.text: Engineered to perform in challenging conditions, the USW-Mission-Critical operates reliably within a wide temperature range of -5°C to +40°C. It supports universal AC input from 100 to 240V and external 48V DC battery inputs for flexible power options and redundancy. The system can deliver a maximum power consumption of 240W including PoE and AC output, while consuming just 50W without PoE. Its rack-mount 1U form factor fits easily into existing infrastructure. This switch complies with NDAA requirements, ensuring security and reliability for government and enterprise use. The integrated battery backup and intelligent power management provide peace of mind by maintaining critical network devices and connected services even during extended power failures, making it a top choice for mission-critical networking environments.
